level_signals: at least 3 years at a Staff or Senior PM level
Comp range $213,600–$300,000 consistent with Staff IC at a high-growth AI startup
Role title is explicitly 'Staff Product Manager'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of product management experience, with at least 3 years at a Staff or Senior PM level at a high-growth technology company.
at least 3 years at a Staff or Senior PM level at a high-growth technology company.
Proven track record of owning and scaling complex platform or infrastructure products, ideally in enterprise SaaS, document management, knowledge management, or data-intensive domains.
Strong technical acumen with the ability to engage deeply with engineering on system design, distributed systems, data pipelines, and retrieval architectures.
Experience building products that handle sensitive data with robust security, compliance, and access control requirements.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to influence stakeholders at various levels, from engineers to executives.
Demonstrated ability to thrive in ambiguous, fast-paced environments and drive clarity through complexity.
Strong product sense and attention to detail-ability to think through both high-level strategy and nitty-gritty implementation details.

scope.surface: New product at intersection of AI, legal data, and legal work — including Harvey application, Word Add-In, Outlook plugin, integrations, APIs, and data partnerships
scope.business_model: enterprise B2B SaaS, AI platform for legal and professional services
primary_metrics_inferred: Integration adoption and customer value delivered
Commercial impact aligned with GTM strategy
core_responsibilities: Define and execute the product strategy for Harvey's new product across the Harvey application, Word Add-In, Outlook plugin, and beyond
Work closely with the Integrations engineering pod and Partnerships team to prioritize, scope, and ship integrations that drive customer value and competitive differentiation
Translate partner and customer demand signals into a coherent product roadmap
Collaborate closely with Engineering, GTM, Customer Success, and PMM to ensure investments are aligned with commercial strategy and customer needs
Partner with GTM and Partnerships to translate new capabilities into sales enablement, positioning, and customer-facing narratives
Define success metrics for health and iterate based on data

green_flags: Clear product surface and 0→1 ownership with named surfaces (Word Add-In, Outlook plugin)
Harvey has strong product-market fit signals: 1500+ customers in 60+ countries
World-class investor support mentioned, signals financial stability
Role is genuinely strategic — not execution-only
red_flags: Location and work authorization not stated anywhere in the JD — remote/hybrid/onsite and visa sponsorship status completely unclear
No equity details provided despite likely being a significant part of total comp at this level
Very broad comp range ($213,600–$300,000) may suggest high location pay differential or wide level band
No 'nice to have' section means all requirements are stated as hard — high bar with limited flexibility signaled

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from role title + years + comp range)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no people management mentioned)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'Depending on your location' language in privacy notice footer)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated)
compensation.equity_mentioned (inferred as false; not referenced in JD)
ambiguities: The JD mentions 'ideally in enterprise SaaS, document management, knowledge management, or data-intensive domains' under the platform/infrastructure track record requirement — this reads as a soft qualifier (ideally) but is nested within an otherwise hard requirement. Treated as context/flavoring for a hard requirement rather than a separate nice-to-have.

Location is entirely absent from the JD body. The footer references an Applicant Privacy Notice 'depending on your location' which implies multiple geographies are possible, but no city, region, or remote eligibility is specified.
